steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence shifts from praising Jones as a writer to explaining why featuring Blackfeet characters matters — specifically that it ensures Indigenous people have a place in the horror genre. The question asks what function this sentence serves in the paragraph.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It predicts that non-Indigenous authors will include more Indigenous characters in their work.: the sentence describes an existing cultural impact, not what Jones will do next. ✗
  • Choice B — It challenges the widely held view that Stephen Graham Jones based *My Heart Is a Chainsaw* on events in his own life.: the passage makes no autobiographical claim to challenge. ✗
  • Choice C — It highlights the significance of the portrayal of Indigenous people in *My Heart Is a Chainsaw*.: the sentence explains that the novel's portrayal of Indigenous people opened representational space in the genre — a statement of cultural importance. ✓
  • Choice D — It asks why so many readers who don't enjoy the horror genre have purchased *My Heart Is a Chainsaw*.: the sentence is a statement, not a question, and the passage raises no such inquiry. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
